summary: @shadanai/openclaw ships a remote-control backdoor: hardcoded gateway token, disabled device auth, author-origin CORS allowlist, and silent prompt-relay
details: This package impersonates the legitimate `openclaw` project (its README is a verbatim copy directing users to `npm install -g openclaw@latest`) and the postinstall script creates a `node_modules/openclaw -> @shadanai/openme` symlink so imports of `openclaw/...` resolve to this fork. The postinstall (`scripts/postinstall.mjs`) then installs a complete remote-control surface into every installer's machine: (1) writes a hardcoded shared gateway token `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=276f6078...aaef75` into `~/.openclaw/.env` and bakes it into `gateway.json`, identical for every installer; (2) writes the gateway config with `dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth: true`, `dangerouslyAllowHostHeaderOriginFallback: true`, and an `allowedOrigins` list including `https://im.shadanai.com` and `https://shadanai.com`, so author-controlled web pages can authenticate to the installer's local OpenClaw gateway and drive its control-plane RPCs (including shell-exec tooling); (3) silently runs `mkcert -install` to inject a new root CA into the OS/browser trust stores; (4) executes `npx clawhub@latest install sonoscli` with a mutable `@latest` tag, giving the author a future remote-update channel to ship arbitrary code to all installers without republishing this package. At runtime, the bundled `gateway.json` hardcodes the default model provider to `https://one-api.shadanai.com/v1` with a hardcoded API key (`sk-xRxGLt...D9`) and sets `agents.defaults.model` to `shadan/glm-5`, silently relaying every prompt and response through the author's proxy. After box-im login, `extensions/box-im/src/owner-bootstrap.ts` calls `registerDevice(apiUrl, auth.tokenKey, reg)` against `one-api.shadanai.com` and uploads the installer's gateway token, port, and a deterministic device ID — closing the loop so the author's backend can drive the installer's local gateway. The package additionally redistributes a third-party Z.ai API key (`ZAI_API_KEY=b0952b46...bLM5`) to every installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
